---
title: "VirtualShip Classroom Advisory Group meeting"
subtitle: "11th May 2026"
format:
    revealjs:
        self-contained: true
        scrollable: true
        slide-number: true
        theme: default
        logo: "https://virtualship.readthedocs.io/en/latest/_static/virtual_ship_logo.png"
        controls: true
        include-in-header:
            - text: |
                <style>
                    #title-slide .title {
                    font-size: 1.4em;
                    font-weight: 600;
                    }
                </style>
                <style>
                    #title-slide .subtitle {
                    font-size: 1.0em;
                    }
                </style>

---

## Agenda

1. Updates
2. Gliders
3. Gamification
4. AOB

## Team composition

<!-- TODO: add photos? -->

- New team members:
    - Jamie (Postdoc)
    - Gonçalo (Research Officer)


## VirtualShip Classroom in new courses {.smaller}

**At UU...**

- In total 4 courses at UU (8 cycles), with implementation in each to continue in 2026/2027.
- Implementation has been extended to two new courses:
    - Ocean of the Future [**BSc**; Geosciences]
    - Sustainable Water Resources Management [MSc; Geosciences]
        - 4 hour 'masterclass'

![DBR cycles at UU; Daniels et al. _In prep_.](DBRcycles.png){height=200px}

## VirtualShip Classroom in new courses {.smaller}

**Beyond UU... !**

- €100k NKO funding to implement the VirtualShip Classroom at four new universities in the Netherlands:
    - TU Delft
    - University of Amsterdam
    - Wageningen University and Research
    - University of Groningen

- Mixture of BSc and MSc courses, ranging from ecology to physics.
- Implementation to start in 2026/2027, for two academic years.
- A transition from us actively supporting the implementation at each university to a more hands-off approach.

## Student evaluation (at UU){.smaller style="font-size: 0.6em;"}

<br>
Across 8 cycles of teaching (pooled results)...

![Across 8 cycles of teaching at UU; Atkins et al. _Submitted_](evaluation.png){height=100%}

## Updates to the software {.smaller}

The software has updated over the last year... often in line with evolving user needs and feedback from students.

![](DBR2_figure.png){height=100%}

## Student evaluation (at UU){.smaller style="font-size: 0.6em;"}

**Software** evaluation from students' perspective is less positive.

![From Dynamical Oceanography 2025/2026 (**MSc**)](software_evaluation.png){height=100%}


## Student evaluation (at UU){.smaller style="font-size: 0.6em;"}

And even more challenging for **BSc** students with mixed programming experience.

![From Ocean of the Future 2025/2026 (**BSc**)](software_evaluation_bsc.png){height=100%}

## VR / 360° videos

- 2024/2025: shorter videos, ship tours, scientist interviews and one instrument-focused video (ADCP).
- **2025/2026**: 4 new longer videos (more instruments, day-at-sea videos) from DUST expedition that Emma and Gonçalo participated in.
    - Newer videos after Gonçalo had 7 months more training in editing skills.
- New bespoke video player application designed with the AV team at UU.
    - Focusing on ability to deploy videos to students asynchronously.

## VR / 360° videos {.smaller style="font-size: 0.6em;"}

{{< video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AiuHntw3rmk&t=115s height="600" width="100%" >}}

## VR / 360° videos

<br>

![Across 8 cycles of teaching at UU](evaluation_vr.png){height=100%}

With more in-depth evaluation in Bhagat _et al._ (in review).

## Publications {.smaller style="font-size: 0.6em;"}

**Published**:

<div style="position: relative; display: inline-block; width: 100%;">
1.  Daniels, E., Chytas, C. and van Sebille, E. 2025. The Virtual Ship Classroom: Developing Virtual Fieldwork as an Authentic Learning Environment for Physical Oceanography. _Current:  The Journal of Marine Eduction_. 40(2), p. 44–57. https://doi.org/10.5334/cjme.121.
<div class="fragment fade-in" data-fragment-index="1" style="position: absolute; top: -6px; left: -10px; right: -10px; bottom: -6px; border: 3px solid #40abedbc; background: rgba(98, 177, 234, 0.12); border-radius: 6px; pointer-events: none; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;"><span style="color: #000000ff; font-weight: bold; font-size: 1.2em; background: white; padding: 4px 10px; border-radius: 4px;">Evaluation of first two DBR cycles in teaching at UU.</span></div>
</div>


**Submitted/in review**:

<div style="position: relative; display: inline-block; width: 100%;">
2.  Bhagat, S. , Daniels, E. and Veldkamp, A. Authentic Learning in Marine Science Education Using Immersive 360˚ Videos. _Journal of Geoscience Education_.
<div class="fragment fade-in" data-fragment-index="2" style="position: absolute; top: -6px; left: -10px; right: -10px; bottom: -6px; border: 3px solid #40abedbc; background: rgba(98, 177, 234, 0.12); border-radius: 6px; pointer-events: none; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;"><span style="color: #000000ff; font-weight: bold; font-size: 1.2em; background: white; padding: 4px 10px; border-radius: 4px;">Evaluation of __VR__ in first two DBR cycles in teaching at UU.</span></div>
</div>

<div style="position: relative; display: inline-block; width: 100%;">
3.  Atkins, J., Daniels, E., Hodgskin, N., Stuurman, A., Simoes-Sousa, I. and van Sebille, E. VirtualShip: A Python package for simulating oceanographic fieldwork in the global ocean. _The Journal of Open Source Software_.
<div class="fragment fade-in" data-fragment-index="3" style="position: absolute; top: -6px; left: -10px; right: -10px; bottom: -6px; border: 3px solid #40abedbc; background: rgba(98, 177, 234, 0.12); border-radius: 6px; pointer-events: none; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;"><span style="color: #000000ff; font-weight: bold; font-size: 1.2em; background: white; padding: 4px 10px; border-radius: 4px;">Presenting and describing the VirtualShip Python package.</span></div>
</div>

<div style="position: relative; display: inline-block; width: 100%;">
4.  Atkins, J., Daniels, E. and van Sebille, E. The VirtualShip Classroom: Simulating Fieldwork in the Global Ocean. _Oceanography: Education_.
<div class="fragment fade-in" data-fragment-index="4" style="position: absolute; top: -6px; left: -10px; right: -10px; bottom: -6px; border: 3px solid #40abedbc; background: rgba(98, 177, 234, 0.12); border-radius: 6px; pointer-events: none; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;"><span style="color: #000000ff; font-weight: bold; font-size: 1.2em; background: white; padding: 4px 10px; border-radius: 4px;">For teachers: overview & deploying the VSC in class.</span></div>
</div>

**In prep**:

<div style="position: relative; display: inline-block; width: 100%;">
5.  Daniels, E., Atkins, J. and van Sebille, E. Design-based research about virtual oceanography fieldwork as an authentic learning environment. _Journal of Computing in Higher Education_.
<div class="fragment fade-in" data-fragment-index="5" style="position: absolute; top: -6px; left: -10px; right: -10px; bottom: -6px; border: 3px solid #40abedbc; background: rgba(98, 177, 234, 0.12); border-radius: 6px; pointer-events: none; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;"><span style="color: #000000ff; font-weight: bold; font-size: 1.2em; background: white; padding: 4px 10px; border-radius: 4px;">Evaluation & insights from eight DBR cycles (incl. derived design principles).</span></div>
</div>

## Other updates

- We have partnered with the National Marine Facilities (NMF) to join their 'roadshow' (focused around new AWvB ship) and promote VirtualShip to new users.
- VU Amsterdam, TU Delft, Radboud University, University of Groningen and UU.

## What's next?

- Implementation at new universities as part of NKO scale-up project.
- Continued development of the software, VR and teaching material.
- Continued evaluation of resources and implementations.
- ... which leads us to the next agenda items!

## Discussion point: Gliders

_How to integrate and work with gliders in the VirtualShip Classroom?_

## Discussion point: Gamification

_How can/should we incorporate gamification into the VirtualShip Classroom?_

_How does it relate to authenticity?_
